verses.text: Solomon determines to build a house for the name of the LORD, and sends to Huram of Tyre for help. The temple is the great work of his reign. To build a place for the worship of God was the worthiest use of his peace and wealth.

Solomon determines to build a house for the name of the LORD, and sends to Huram of Tyre for help. The temple is the great work of his reign. To build a place for the worship of God was the worthiest use of his peace and wealth.

Solomon determines to build a house for the name of the LORD, and sends to Huram of Tyre for help. The temple is the great work of his reign. To build a place for the worship of God was the worthiest use of his peace and wealth.

Solomon explains the house is for incense, offerings, and feasts, yet marvels: 'who is able to build him an house, seeing the heaven... cannot contain him? who am I?' The infinite God needs no dwelling. The temple is for worship, built in humble awe of His greatness.

Solomon explains the house is for incense, offerings, and feasts, yet marvels: 'who is able to build him an house, seeing the heaven... cannot contain him? who am I?' The infinite God needs no dwelling. The temple is for worship, built in humble awe of His greatness.

Solomon explains the house is for incense, offerings, and feasts, yet marvels: 'who is able to build him an house, seeing the heaven... cannot contain him? who am I?' The infinite God needs no dwelling. The temple is for worship, built in humble awe of His greatness.

Solomon asks Huram for a skilled craftsman and cedar timber, promising generous provision for the workers. He seeks the best skill and deals fairly with labour. What is built for God deserves excellence, and those who work for it deserve just reward.

Solomon asks Huram for a skilled craftsman and cedar timber, promising generous provision for the workers. He seeks the best skill and deals fairly with labour. What is built for God deserves excellence, and those who work for it deserve just reward.

Huram answers in writing, blessing 'the LORD God of Israel, that made heaven and earth,' for giving David a wise son. A Gentile king praises the true God. The LORD draws even outsiders to honour His name and serve the building of His house.

Huram answers in writing, blessing 'the LORD God of Israel, that made heaven and earth,' for giving David a wise son. A Gentile king praises the true God. The LORD draws even outsiders to honour His name and serve the building of His house.

Huram answers in writing, blessing 'the LORD God of Israel, that made heaven and earth,' for giving David a wise son. A Gentile king praises the true God. The LORD draws even outsiders to honour His name and serve the building of His house.

Huram sends a master craftsman, son of a Danite woman and a Tyrian, skilled in every work, and agrees to supply timber by sea. Like Bezaleel, a gifted man of mixed descent serves God's house. The LORD uses skill from every quarter for His work.

Huram sends a master craftsman, son of a Danite woman and a Tyrian, skilled in every work, and agrees to supply timber by sea. Like Bezaleel, a gifted man of mixed descent serves God's house. The LORD uses skill from every quarter for His work.

Solomon numbers the resident foreigners and sets them to bear burdens, hew stone, and oversee the work. The great house is built by the ordered labour of many. No work for God is done by a few; it takes a multitude, each in his place.
